study_group_representative.quote: In this study, the number of daily confirmed cases of COVID-19 in the selected countries were obtained from the Center for Systems Science and Engineering (CSSE) at Johns Hopkins University

intervention_and_participants_defined.quote: In this study, we estimated a time-varying reproduction number, Rt, to assess the effectiveness of control measures employed in 10 selected counties.

outcome_assessed_for_60pct.quote: First, the estimation of Rt was based on the reported case number only.

follow_up_length_reported.quote: Figure 1K shows the reduction in Rt (ΔRt) in each country after the control measures have been implemented for 3 weeks.

outcome_definition_objective_precise.quote: The time-varying reproduction number, Rt, is the average number of secondary cases of disease caused by an infected individual over his or her infectious period.

important_prognostic_factors_accounted_f.quote: Finally, country-to-country comparisons of the effectiveness of control measures are problematic due to many confounding factors, such as different population sizes and densities, superspreading events, social mixing patterns and nuances in social distancing measures.

analysis_described_and_effect_quantified.quote: We employed a method proposed by Cori et al.1 to estimate Rt in 10 selected countries... We also calculated the reduction in Rt (ΔRt): a higher ΔRt is indicative of more successful control measures.
